articles.description: Arsenal move five points clear in the Premier League title race but is their reliance on set-pieces an issue?
articles.title: Arsenal: Ugly or a tactical masterclass? Gunners set-pieces prove decisive again

articles.description: With six wins and a draw from seven games in charge of Manchester United, would it prove more of a gamble not to give Michael Carrick the head coach position full-time?
articles.title: Michael Carrick: Would it be more of a gamble by Man Utd not to appoint him  full-time?

articles.description: India secure a T20 World Cup semi-final showdown with England after a five-wicket victory over West Indies thanks to Sanju Samson's scintillating unbeaten 97.
articles.title: T20 World Cup 2026 results: Sanju Samson's 97 gives India win over Windies and sets up England semi

articles.description: What does the US conflict with Iran mean for what was already set to be a highly politicised World Cup this summer?
articles.title: World Cup 2026: What does the Middle East conflict mean for the tournament?

articles.description: Benfica boss Jose Mourinho says Gianluca Prestianni's career under him will be "over" if it is found he racially abused Vinicius Jr.
articles.title: Jose Mourinho says Gianluca Prestianni will not play for him again if he racially abused Vinicius Jr

articles.description: Without a win in 10 Premier League matches – their worst league run in 22 years – Tottenham are facing a battle to stay up.
articles.title: Tudor fury at 'home referee' as Spurs left in 'serious emergency'

articles.description: Amid one of the most captivating title fights in the history Scotland's top flight, BBC Scotland looks at the state of play for each of the four clubs involved.
articles.title: Scottish Premiership: What's the state of play in 'epic' four-way title race?

articles.description: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?
articles.title: Troy Deeney's Team of the Week: Pickford, Branthwaite, Saliba, Wilson, Fernandes, Ekitike

articles.description: Players who cover their mouths when speaking to opponents during confrontations should be sent off, says Gianni Infantino.
articles.title: Footballers covering their mouths should be sent off - Gianni Infantino

articles.description: A dominant afternoon session set Barry Hawkins on the way to winning his first Welsh Open title, beating Jack Lisowski 9-5 in the final.
articles.title: Barry Hawkins beats Jack Lisowski to win first Welsh Open title
